subject: DevOps
title: Building Effective Dashboards
description: A micro-lesson that makes Building Effective Dashboards usable.

hook.text: The dashboard had 47 graphs. Nobody could find the one that mattered during an outage.

buildup.text: Dashboards should answer a question at a glance. Too many panels creates confusion.

discovery.text: Start with the four golden signals on one page. Drill down to detail from there.

twist.text: Different audiences need different dashboards. Execs want SLOs. Engineers want latency histograms.

climax.text: Use consistent colors and layouts. Red means bad, green means good. Don't reinvent conventions.

punchline.text: One glance should tell the story. Keep dashboards focused.

quiz.question: What makes an effective dashboard?
options: It answers a specific question at a glance with focused panels
It displays as many metrics as possible
It uses unique colors and layouts for each panel
